articles.title: WFH SHOULD BE COMFORTABLE.
articles.summary: Working from your kitchen was fine for 2020. But in 2025, you shouldn't be hunched and achy on your WFH days.

articles.title: 🏷️ Get 65% off the FlexiSpot Ergonomic Office Chair
GOOGLE WON'T HAVE TO SELL CHROME OR ANDROID, BUT HAS TO SHARE SEARCH DATA
articles.summary: Google avoided a forced sell-off of Chrome or Android after the court ruled these products were not directly used to maintain its search monopoly. However, the ruling introduces a major shift by requiring Google to share parts of its search index and user-interaction data with qualified competitors. This opens the door for rivals to improve their own search products, potentially impacting search ad performance and traffic dynamics over time.

articles.title: HOW OFTEN DO AI ASSISTANTS HALLUCINATE LINKS?
articles.summary: AI assistants send visitors to 404 pages 2.87X more often than Google search. ChatGPT had the highest 404 rates, with 1.01% of clicked URLs and 2.38% of cited URLs returning errors, compared to Google's 0.15% and 0.84%. Claude, Copilot, Perplexity, and Gemini had lower error rates, with Mistral being the lowest, but based on the smallest sample. Many errors stem from outdated links or hallucinated URLs that mimic real site structures but never existed. The study suggests using analytics to identify these links and either redirecting traffic or improving 404 pages to help visitors.

articles.title: RETAIL PANIC: WHAT THE END OF THE ‘DE MINIMIS' EXEMPTION MEANS FOR BRANDS ACROSS THE GLOBE
articles.summary: The sudden end of the US de minimis exemption, which allowed shipments under $800 to enter duty-free, is disrupting global retail supply chains. It will raise costs for businesses and increase prices for consumers. Initially set to expire in 2027, the policy was abruptly terminated by executive order, impacting companies from small Etsy sellers to major brands like Tapestry and Lululemon that had built business models around it. Used by e-commerce giants like Shein and Temu, the exemption's removal is forcing businesses to restructure logistics, delay shipments, and, in some cases, halt US sales.

articles.title: THIS DEV TIKTOK FORMAT KEEPS GOING VIRAL
articles.summary: Buildspace founder Farza is growing Tidbit by using TikTok to share build-in-public and dev-focused content. It currently has 4.3M views across 40 videos driving 16% of signups. The most viral post used the hook, “Just noticed my coworker coding. NO Cursor. NO Windsurf. He's just sitting there typing code manually and reading documentation. What a psychopath”, a format that has also gone viral in other niches. Build-in-public content shouldn't be underestimated as a growth driver for early-stage prosumer and B2B products.

articles.title: THE COUNTERINTUITIVE MODEL: THE GROWTH MODEL THAT OUTPERFORMS FREEMIUM AND FREE TRIALS
articles.summary: Most SaaS startups struggle with free users converting, as freemium models only convert about 2-5% and traditional trials lose around 75% of signups after expiration. A reverse trial gives new users full premium access for a limited period, then downgrades them to a free tier if they do not upgrade, combining acquisition benefits of freemium with conversion benefits of trials. Companies like Canva, Toggl Track, and Grammarly use this model successfully, with Toggl Track reporting doubled premium revenue after switching. Reverse trials work best when the key value sits behind paid features, onboarding is complex, or standout features need early exposure.

articles.title: SHOPIFY SEO TIP: HOW GOOGLE CRAWLS PRODUCTS TO MAP RANKINGS AND CLICKS
articles.summary: Google treats the first few products in a Shopify collection as “signature items”. This influences Shopping rankings, crawl priority, and snippets like “Popular products”. To increase SEO visibility, keep evergreen, keyword-rich SKUs in the top spots, add structured data for reviews and pricing, rotate seasonal items without displacing anchors, and align top products with SKUs already surfaced in SERPs.

articles.title: DISTRIBUTION BEFORE PRODUCT: THE OPERATOR'S 90-DAY GTM PLAYBOOK - WITH AI PROMPTS
articles.summary: Most startups fail because they lack distribution, not because their products are bad. Customer acquisition costs have risen 300-400% in the past 3 years, stretching CAC payback periods to 18-24 months, which makes traditional paid marketing unsustainable. Distribution-first companies succeed by building owned channels, audiences, and credibility before launching products, using tactics like waitlists, viral loops, and shareable templates. Case studies such as Suno, Robinhood, and Notion show that strategies like community-driven growth, gamified referrals, and viral content can drive rapid adoption. A structured 90-day playbook helps founders build compounding distribution assets that reduce CAC by at least 40% and create defensible growth advantages.

articles.title: HOW TO QUICKLY CREATE QUALITY MUSIC AND VIDEO
articles.summary: Music platforms like Udio and Suno allow marketers to define tone, instruments, and vocals with simple text prompts. Visuals begin with quick image generation in Midjourney and evolve into animated assets that fit the overall concept with tools like Runway. By starting with music, teams can set the emotional and structural foundation for the final product, then assemble everything in editing software like Premiere to ensure quality and consistency.

articles.title: SEARCH BREATHES NEW LIFE INTO ORGANIC SOCIAL FOR BRANDS
articles.summary: Social media is being used as a search tool rather than just for passive scrolling. In fact, 24% of TikTok users search within 30 seconds of opening the app. Search allows brands to capture intent-driven engagement, while the feed remains more useful for brand building and short-term visibility. Feed content usually lasts up to 7 days, but search content can stay relevant for 9 to 12 months. Brands may benefit from using both approaches, though some may prioritize search depending on their strategic goals.

articles.title: HOW TO LAUNCH YOUR STARTUP OUT OF STEALTH, FROM FIGMA'S FIRST MARKETER
articles.summary: The structure of a successful launch begins with a firm date, which forces prioritization and reveals the most essential product and messaging decisions. From there, creating foundational assets such as a homepage and founder-led announcement post transforms abstract positioning into clear and testable narratives. These materials help align internal stakeholders while offering a consistent message to the market. In the absence of media reach, the best approach is to have the early distribution led by the founder and reinforced through trusted personal networks.
